name: Itxina Massif Loop Trail
name_original: Axkorrigan, Ojo de Atxular y cueva de Superlegor— bucle por el biotopo Protegido de Itxina
description: Hike a difficult 6.6-mile loop through the Itxina Natural Monument, featuring the Ojo de Atxular arch and Cueva de Supelegor.
region_intro: Hiking around Areatza provides access to the diverse landscapes of Gorbeia Natural Park, the largest natural park in the Basque Country. The region is characterized by rugged mountains, including Mount Gorbeia, and extensive forests of beech, oak, and pine. Unique karst formations like the Itxina Massif and wetlands such as Saldropo also define the terrain. Crystal-clear rivers and springs traverse the landscape, offering varied environments for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Areatza The most popular hiking route is Itxina Massif Loop Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete, leading through striking limestone formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit loop from Sustrigi, a difficult 10.1 miles (16.2 km) path. This route offers a challenging ascent to the iconic Gorbeia summit. Local hikers also love the Pagomakurre – Arraba Meadows loop from Itxinako natura monumentua, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through open meadows and forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Areatza is defined by mountain peaks, dense beech forests, and unique karst landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Areatza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Areatza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Areatza?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Areatza, offering a wide range of options from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. You'll find routes suitable for various fitness levels, including 2 easy, 9 moderate, and 10 difficult trails within the Gorbeia Natural Park.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes near Areatza?
A: Hiking around Areatza provides access to the diverse landscapes of Gorbeia Natural Park. You can expect to see rugged mountains, extensive forests of beech, oak, and pine, and unique karst formations like the Itxina Massif. Crystal-clear rivers and springs also traverse the landscape, and during the rainy season, the Uguzpe Waterfall is a beautiful sight.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly walks in the Areatza area?
A: Yes, there are easy walks suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19178919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pagomakurre – Arraba Meadows loop from Itxinako natura monumentua&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.1 km trail that takes less than two hours. The Wetland of Saldropo also offers a shorter, low-difficulty route, perfect for a gentle stroll.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to explore while hiking near Areatza?
A: The region is rich in natural and cultural landmarks. You can explore the unique karst landscape of the Itxina Massif, which includes the striking Ojo de Atxular arch and the Cueva de Supelegor. The summit of Mount Gorbeia, crowned by its iconic 17-meter cross, is another major attraction. Additionally, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3053569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Supelegor Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3889987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltzola Caves&lt;/a&gt;. The medieval town center of Areatza itself is a declared Monumental Ensemble, offering historical palaces and houses.

Q: Is Areatza a good destination for dog-friendly hiking?
A: Gorbeia Natural Park, which Areatza is a gateway to, is generally welcoming to dogs, provided they are kept under control and on a leash in sensitive areas to protect wildlife. Many trails are suitable for hiking with dogs, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty together. Always ensure to follow local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Areatza and Gorbeia Natural Park?
A: The Gorbeia Natural Park offers hiking opportunities year-round. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant with mild temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer is also popular, though some higher altitude trails can be warm. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower-altitude trails, but requires appropriate gear and caution due to potential snow and ice on higher peaks like Mount Gorbeia.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Areatza?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035939734/itxina-massif-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itxina Massif Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderately difficult 9.4 km route that takes around 3 hours and 25 minutes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19178919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pagomakurre – Arraba Meadows loop from Itxinako natura monumentua&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easier option.

Q: Where can I find information about Gorbeia Natural Park in Areatza?
A: The Parketxe Areatza, located in Areatza, serves as the visitor center for Gorbeia Natural Park. It provides valuable information about the park's trails, natural features, and organizes various activities, including guided tourist and environmental routes. It's a great first stop for planning your adventures.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Areatza?
A: Areatza, with its medieval town center, offers options for dining and accommodation. You'll find local cafes and pubs where you can refuel after a hike. Additionally, the region has recreational areas like Upoko Makatza and Landaederra, which provide amenities amidst shaded trees, perfect for a picnic or rest stop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Areatza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to dense beech forests and unique karst formations. The well-marked paths and the sense of immersion in nature within Gorbeia Natural Park are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: How can I reach the trailheads around Areatza using public transport?
A: While specific public transport routes to every trailhead can vary, Areatza itself is accessible by public transport. From the town, some trailheads within Gorbeia Natural Park might be reachable by local bus services or require a short taxi ride. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance for the most up-to-date information on routes connecting Areatza to key starting points like Pagomakurre or Sustrigi.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging hikes available from Areatza?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the ascent to Mount Gorbeia is a popular choice. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit loop from Sustrigi&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.2 km path with significant elevation gain, offering a challenging climb to the iconic summit. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40352319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Itxinako natura monumentua&lt;/a&gt;, which is even longer at 17.8 km.
tips: Natural gateway to the interior of the Itxina Massif and to Basque mythology.
Summit of the Itxina massif in Gorbeia, close to Axkorrigan (1090m), with which it forms the prow of this particular limestone massif.
Summit in the protected area of Itxina. It is the northernmost peak characterized by the limestone cliffs that surround it.

One of the most beautiful caves I have ever seen. First because of the location, second because of the small challenge of not getting lost to get there, and thirdly because it is super cool: a huge mouth and easy to travel (with a flashlight) a good stretch. Absolutely recommended.

Cave inside the so-called Great Central Crack, in the Itzina Massif. Its interior can be accessed by descending the ramp of the natural tunnel from the mouth of Axlegor, municipality of Orozko (Bizkaia).
Beautiful cave easy to cross in the Itxina massif.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Axkorrigan, Ojo de Atxular y cueva de Superlegor loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a difficult hike. It demands good physical fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy hiking shoes. Some sections can be highly technical, featuring rugged terrain with steep stones, potholes, and sinkholes, especially within the Itxina massif.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this loop?
faqs.answer: The moving time for this hike is approximately 3 hours and 37 minutes. However, due to its challenging nature and the desire to explore points of interest like the Ojo de Atxular and Cueva de Supelegor, most hikers should plan for a total duration of 4 to 6 hours, depending on pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Pagomakurre car park, which is a common access point for routes into the Itxina Protected Biotope. This car park serves as a convenient starting and ending point for the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied. You'll traverse charming pine and beech forests, open meadows, and encounter a strong incline towards Ojo de Atxular. The heart of the Itxina massif is rugged, characterized by steep stones, potholes, 'craters,' and sinkholes. It's often referred to as a 'labyrinth' due to its intricate karst landscape.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the impressive natural rock arch, Ojo de Atxular, and the grand Cueva de Supelegor. You'll also pass by Urtutxe Peak (1,090 m) and Axkorrigan Occidental Summit (1095 m), offering great views. Other notable caves like Axlaor Cave and Arkuaxpe are also in the vicinity.

faqs.question: Is navigation difficult on this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, navigation can be challenging, especially within the 'labyrinth' of Itxina. There can be brief sections without a clearly defined path, making a GPS track highly recommended to stay on course.

faqs.question: What should I know about exploring the caves, especially Supelegor?
faqs.answer: The Cueva de Supelegor is an immense and visually striking cave. If you plan to enter, even just the entrance area, you must bring a light source (headlamp or powerful flashlight) as it gets very dark inside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months when the weather is more stable and daylight hours are longer. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, as the terrain can become very slippery and hazardous in wet or icy conditions.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Gorbea Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Itxina Biotope, to protect wildlife. Given the trail's difficult and technical terrain, ensure your dog is fit and accustomed to such conditions, and be prepared to assist them over obstacles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Itxina Protected Biotope?
faqs.answer: No, generally you do not need a specific permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike within the Itxina Protected Biotope or Gorbea Natural Park. However, always respect the natural environment and adhere to any local regulations or signage regarding conservation.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other trails. You will briefly encounter parts of the [CIMA BI02] Larreder - Pagomakurre and Areatza - Pagomakurre routes.

waypoints.name: Axlaor Cave (Itxina Massif, Gorbea Natural Park)
